title: Heegaard Floer homology and the L-space conjecture for graph manifolds
abstract: We verify the L-space conjecture for all graph manifolds, showing that the three equivalent characterisations (Heegaard Floer, taut foliations, left-orderable $\pi_1$) coincide in this class.
ai_proof_review:
## Setup

The paper completes a decade-long program by handling the remaining graph-manifold case. The earlier work of Hanselman–Rasmussen–Watson reduces the question to a combinatorial condition on JSJ pieces.

## Theorem 1.1

The combinatorial condition is verified by a case analysis over Seifert-fibre structures. The case analysis is long (40+ pages) but each case is mechanically checked.

## Section 6 (foliation correspondence)

The taut-foliation side uses a gluing construction. Construction is standard but the verification that the glued foliation is taut on the boundary uses an inequality (Lemma 6.4) whose proof I would want to double-check; the paper proof relies on a numerical condition that I had to recompute.

## Overall

A major result in 3-manifold topology that completes a famous open problem in its named class. The proof is mostly bookkeeping plus one sharp lemma. Confidence is high but not certainty; one technical lemma deserves expert refereeing.
